ArchitectureNotes.objective: Replace the monolithic bot with a contract-first GRACE-governed platform where the Go API owns orchestration and Python workers own heavy execution.
ArchitectureNotes.document-role: This artifact is the GRACE execution and architecture backbone for the migration. It is intentionally detailed enough to replace the original long-form markdown plan rather than merely summarize it.
ArchitectureNotes.non-goal: Do not preserve a long-lived legacy compatibility layer and do not move heavy ML or report logic into Go.
ArchitectureNotes.risk-1: Cross-surface contract drift between API, workers, Web UI, Telegram, and MCP can invalidate large implementation waves if contracts are not frozen first.
ArchitectureNotes.risk-2: Retry, cancel, and webhook delivery can look correct in happy-path demos but regress under partial failure unless they are modeled before code extraction.

section.note: The role of the migration plan as implementation SSOT is preserved.
Current monolith shape, files, storage model, and preserved capabilities are preserved.

section.original: 3. Non-Goals for the First Platform Version
section.note: All explicitly excluded scopes are preserved.
All platform rules and separation principles are preserved.
All fixed migration decisions remain explicit and immutable until superseded.
High-level components and control-plane versus execution-plane split are preserved.
Target repository structure is preserved.
Compose services, network assumptions, and CPU-only whisper shape are preserved.
Core entities, job types, statuses, artifact kinds, and delivery strategies are preserved.
All operational tables, important fields, constraints, and rules are preserved.
Queue design, payload shape, retries, and SkipRetry rules are preserved.
Buckets, object keys, access model, and presigned download behavior are preserved.
Request rules, endpoints, delivery model, idempotency, and error codes are preserved.
Endpoint, event envelope, subscription model, and polling precedence rule are preserved.
Claim-run-finalize lifecycle, internal endpoints, and worker-specific progress models are preserved.
API rules, worker rules, and subprocess handling are preserved.
Retry-as-new-job lineage model is preserved.
Responsibilities for Web UI, Telegram bot, and MCP are preserved.
Required pages, actions, and late-wave admin operations are preserved.
Required tool surface is preserved.
Thin adapter behavior and current UX preservation are preserved.
Required services, volumes, and CPU resource notes are preserved.
Per-service environment contract is preserved.
Current-file to target-module mapping is preserved.
Wave 0 through Wave 9, deliverables, files, and phases are preserved.
Contract, API, worker, UI, Telegram, and MCP verification plans are preserved.
All named risks and mitigation directions are preserved.
Completion criteria are preserved.
Recommended implementation order is preserved as historical guidance.
Do and do-not rules for future implementation sessions are preserved.

file.role: Telegram runtime, source extraction orchestration, attachment download, media-group buffering, callback handlers, status messages, and result delivery
Transcription worker runtime, local source materialization, per-source execution, batch aggregate merging, and transcript artifact persistence
YouTube transcript fast path and Whisper fallback shared by the transcription worker
Transcript/report markdown normalization and DOCX artifact rendering
Single generic AI worker runtime for report and deep-research operations backed by agent_run jobs

current-storage.item: PostgreSQL is the system of record for jobs, events, source sets, API-owned batch drafts, and artifacts
MinIO stores uploaded sources and produced artifacts
Go HTTP API owns orchestration and delivery semantics
Web UI, Telegram adapter, and MCP adapter consume the API boundary

preserved-capabilities.item: Telegram voice, audio, video, and document transcription

DecisionProvenance.decision: Separate HTTP API server selected as the single backend boundary.
Async job model selected rather than synchronous request-response execution.
Separate worker processes execute heavy work; API does not run transcription, report generation, or deep research inline.
Object storage chosen as MinIO in Docker Compose.
PostgreSQL chosen as the persistent source of truth for jobs and artifact metadata.
No auth model in v1 because the stack is local-only.
MCP remains a separate thin adapter rather than being embedded into the API process.
Sources are uploaded through the API in v1 rather than via direct MinIO uploads.
Initial platform version intentionally drops cross-request dedupe and reuse.

Report and deep research remain public operation concepts and child lineage affordances, but their AI execution is modeled as `agent_run` jobs handled by the single worker-agent-runner rather than dedicated worker services.
Project remains self-hosted and local-first without quotas.
Simple React UI is required and lives as a separate monorepo app.
Everything is containerized under Docker Compose including CPU-only Whisper runtime.
Clients choose one delivery strategy per job, polling by default or webhook when explicitly configured.
asynq is the queue substrate.
Artifact downloads use MinIO presigned links rather than API proxying.
Multi-file upload through the API is required in the first version.
There is no legacy compatibility track; the repository is refactored directly into the target architecture.
Go API module granularity is fixed for the migration horizon as internal/api, internal/jobs, internal/storage, internal/queue, internal/ws, and internal/db.
Worker-control contracts remain explicit HTTP routes for the migration and first platform version.
Redis is reserved for asynq queueing only; event delivery is driven from persisted job events, WebSocket broadcast, and webhook dispatch inside the API.

Batch transcription supersedes the old concat-first combined flow: the public route is `/v1/transcription-jobs/batch`, the API creates a root aggregate transcription job plus per-source transcription children, and worker-transcription assembles aggregate artifacts through `job:transcription.aggregate`.

Pre-submit Telegram batch baskets are API-owned batch drafts with draft_id, version, owner scope, ordered items, and open/submitted/expired/canceled status. Telegram callback data carries draft_id and version or a draft-scoped item token; adapter-local durable basket state and chat/user keyed `basket:start` callbacks are stale compatibility inputs, not canonical processing inputs.

component.role: Go HTTP API and WebSocket server
Single Python AI worker for report, deep-research, and future generic agent_run operations
React UI for job creation, monitoring, and downloads
Thin Python bot adapter over the HTTP API
Thin MCP adapter over the HTTP API
OpenAPI definitions, JSON schemas, WS envelopes, webhook envelopes, and shared enums
Typed client for Web UI and MCP server
Typed Python client for Telegram bot, workers, and local internal tooling
Outbound callback delivery owned by the API server

control-plane-responsibilities.item: accept uploads and source creation requests
create, read, mutate, clear, cancel, expire, and submit pre-submit batch drafts
resolve delivery strategy polling or webhook for create requests
deliver outbound webhooks on job lifecycle changes
expose artifact metadata and audience-scoped MinIO presigned download links
enforce idempotency and child-job reuse semantics

execution-plane-responsibilities.item: execute report generation through `agent_run` operation envelopes
execute deep research generation through `agent_run` operation envelopes
emit progress updates back to the API

notes.note-1: The repository is restructured directly into the target monorepo shape.
notes.note-2: There is no long-lived legacy subtree and no compatibility branch inside the target structure.
notes.note-3: Existing Python modules may remain briefly while being actively moved, but the plan assumes direct replacement rather than prolonged coexistence.

InputShapes.shape: single-source transcription job backed by a one-item source set
batch transcription root backed by a `batch-transcription.v1` source manifest, stable source labels, per-source transcription children, and a root aggregate task
generic agent execution job backed by `harness_name`, prompt or request payload, and referenced prior artifacts

rules.rule-1: Each job chooses exactly one delivery strategy.
rules.rule-3: Webhook can be requested only at job creation and requires a callback URL.
rules.rule-4: The API always returns job identifiers and initial snapshots even when webhook is selected.
rules.rule-5: Polling endpoints remain available as recovery and read access even for webhook-configured jobs.

Enums.enum: queued, running, cancel_requested, succeeded, failed, canceled
transcript_plain, transcript_segmented_markdown, transcript_docx, report_markdown, report_docx, deep_research_markdown, execution_log, agent_result_json, source_manifest_json, batch_diagnostics_json
transcription_upload, transcription_url, transcription_batch, report_create, deep_research_create, agent_run_create, job_retry

Table.name: job_submissions
Table.purpose: Represent idempotent create requests including multi-file upload cases that cannot be modeled safely on jobs alone.

columns.column: created_at timestamptz not null default now()

constraints.constraint: unique on submission_kind and idempotency_key
index on created_at descending

rules.rule: One submission may create many jobs for POST /v1/transcription-jobs.
Same submission key plus same normalized request returns the original accepted response.
Same submission key plus different normalized request returns idempotency_conflict.
`transcription_batch` creates one root aggregate transcription job plus one per-source transcription child per stable source label.
`agent_run_create` creates one root `agent_run` job keyed by `harness_name` and normalized request payload.

Table.name: sources
Table.purpose: Persist uploaded-file, Telegram-upload, and YouTube URL source metadata.

columns.column: created_at timestamptz not null default now()

constraints.constraint: youtube_url rows require source_url and do not require object_key
uploaded_file and telegram_upload rows require object_key
object_key unique where not null

rules.rule: YouTube sources may exist without MinIO objects in v1.
Sources are reusable input records; jobs reference source sets rather than direct source_id after the source-set migration.

Table.name: source_sets
Table.purpose: Represent ordered input sets for transcription, batch aggregate roots, batch source children, and request-only agent runs.

columns.column: created_at timestamptz not null default now()

constraints.constraint: input_kind is constrained to single_source, combined_upload, combined_telegram, batch_transcription, or agent_run in current implementation space

rules.rule: Single-file transcription jobs use a one-item source set.
Historical combined transcription jobs use a multi-item ordered source set only as legacy lineage.
Current batch transcription roots use `input_kind=batch_transcription`; `agent_run` source sets contain no source items.

Table.name: source_set_items
Table.purpose: Persist the ordered list of sources that belong to one source set.

columns.column: source_set_id uuid not null references source_sets on delete cascade
source_id uuid not null references sources
created_at timestamptz not null default now()

constraints.constraint: unique on source_set_id and position
unique on source_set_id and source_label where source_label is not null
index on source_set_id ascending

rules.rule: position defines the final transcription order.
The same source may appear in only one position within one source set in normal API flows.
Batch source labels must match the contract shape, be unique within the source set, and carry `source_label_version=batch-transcription.v1`.

Table.name: batch_drafts
Table.purpose: Represent API-owned pre-submit source-set drafts used by Telegram and any other adapter that needs callback-safe ordered batch assembly before job creation.

columns.column: status text not null check in open, submitted, expired, canceled
created_at timestamptz not null default now()
updated_at timestamptz not null default now()

constraints.constraint: submitted status requires submitted_root_job_id and submitted_at
canceled status requires canceled_at
indexes on owner_type plus owner_chat_id plus owner_user_id plus status, status plus expires_at, and submitted_root_job_id

rules.rule: Drafts are the only canonical pre-submit Telegram batch state; Redis, adapter-local memory, and callback text are not durable sources of truth.
Every mutation checks owner scope and expected version, increments version on success, and returns the current draft snapshot for button rendering.
Submitted, expired, and canceled drafts are terminal for mutation; stale callbacks return explicit submitted, expired, canceled, version_conflict, or owner_mismatch outcomes.
Repeated submit for a submitted draft returns the same submitted_root_job_id or submitted draft response instead of creating another root job.

Table.name: batch_draft_items
Table.purpose: Represent ordered draft sources before promotion into source_sets and source_set_items.

columns.column: draft_id uuid not null references batch_drafts on delete cascade
created_at timestamptz not null default now()

constraints.constraint: unique on draft_id and position
unique on draft_id and source_label
index on draft_id ascending plus position ascending

rules.rule: position defines the later batch source order and must be preserved when submit builds `ordered_source_labels`.
source_ref stores only API-owned references to uploaded temporary source objects or validated URL source data; it must not require bot-local files at submit time.
Submit promotes items atomically into the existing batch source-set, root job, per-source children, and aggregate queue flow.

Table.name: jobs
Table.purpose: Represent root jobs, child jobs, retries, delivery strategy, progress, and terminal outcomes.

columns.column: root_job_id uuid not null references jobs
source_set_id uuid not null references source_sets
delivery_strategy text not null default polling
progress_stage text not null default queued
progress_message text not null default empty
params jsonb not null default empty object
created_at timestamptz not null default now()

constraints.constraint: webhook rows require webhook_url, polling rows do not carry webhook_url
cancel_requested rows require cancel_requested_at
indexes on root_job_id, parent_job_id, retry_of_job_id, source_set_id, job_type plus status
partial unique canonical child constraint by parent_job_id and job_type for non-retry active or completed rows

rules.rule: Initial transcription job has root_job_id equal to id, no parent_job_id, and no retry_of_job_id.
Retry jobs keep root_job_id and parent_job_id but set retry_of_job_id.
Report jobs point to transcription parents; deep-research jobs point to report parents.
Initial `agent_run` jobs are root jobs created by `agent_run_create`; any later child-link variant requires an explicit follow-up packet and must not be inferred implicitly.
Batch transcription roots are aggregate transcription jobs with root_job_id equal to id; per-source transcription children point to the root through parent_job_id and share the same root_job_id.
Batch aggregate retries enqueue only the aggregate task for the root lineage; source-child retries remain explicit child retries.
Only one canonical non-retry child per parent and job_type exists in v1; reruns happen through retry rows.
Retries and child jobs reuse the same source_set_id as their parent lineage.

Table.name: job_artifacts
Table.purpose: Persist output artifact metadata after successful MinIO object writes.

columns.column: job_id uuid not null references jobs on delete cascade
created_at timestamptz not null default now()

constraints.constraint: unique job_id and artifact_kind
unique object_key

rules.rule: source_original is not modeled as a job artifact in v1 because uploaded originals already live in sources.
Create rows only after object upload succeeds.
Transcription success requires transcript_plain, transcript_segmented_markdown, and transcript_docx.
Batch aggregate root success additionally requires source_manifest_json and batch_diagnostics_json.
Report success requires report_markdown and report_docx.
Deep-research success requires deep_research_markdown.

Table.name: job_events
Table.purpose: Append-only timeline and delivery trigger store keyed by job version.

columns.column: job_id uuid not null references jobs on delete cascade
root_job_id uuid not null references jobs
created_at timestamptz not null default now()

constraints.constraint: unique on job_id and version
indexes on job_id ascending and root_job_id ascending timelines

rules.rule: Timeline events are append-only.
One job version maps to one persisted event row.
Webhook deliveries reference job_events rather than mutable job rows.

Table.name: webhook_deliveries
Table.purpose: Persist bounded webhook retry state across API restarts.

columns.column: job_event_id uuid not null unique references job_events on delete cascade
job_id uuid not null references jobs on delete cascade
state text not null default pending
next_attempt_at timestamptz not null default now()
created_at timestamptz not null default now()

constraints.constraint: indexes on state plus next_attempt_at and job_id plus created_at

rules.rule: Create one row per emitted job_event when delivery_strategy equals webhook.
No separate per-attempt history table is required in v1; attempt_count plus last outcome is operationally enough.

QueueContract.PayloadShape: Minimal payload is job_id plus attempt number. Full mutable job metadata must not be placed in asynq payloads.

OwnershipRules.rule-1: The API owns business retry semantics even though asynq performs transport retries.
OwnershipRules.rule-2: asynq retries are only for transient infrastructure failures.
OwnershipRules.rule-3: Deterministic business failures must use SkipRetry rather than blind queue repetition.

SkipRetryCases.case: claim rejected because job is already terminal or already owned
pipeline or model failure that should surface to the user as terminal failed

AccessModel.rule-1: Clients do not upload directly to MinIO in v1.
AccessModel.rule-2: API stores source files in MinIO.
AccessModel.rule-4: Clients receive MinIO presigned download links from the API and download directly from those links.
AccessModel.rule-5: YouTube sources may exist only as DB rows and worker-local temporary materialization in v1.

PresignedLinks.rule: Presigned URLs are generated by the API at read time and must be treated as ephemeral.
Job snapshots and artifact metadata remain available from the API even when URLs expire.

CommonRequestRules.rule-2: Create endpoints accept delivery strategy. If delivery strategy is webhook, a webhook URL is required.
CommonRequestRules.rule-3: Equivalent request plus same Idempotency-Key returns the original accepted response rather than creating new jobs.
CommonRequestRules.rule-5: Create-child behavior is stronger than idempotency: if a canonical child already exists, return it even without an idempotency key.
CommonRequestRules.rule-6: Multipart request normalization includes scalar fields and ordered file manifest with filename, size_bytes, and sha256.
CommonRequestRules.rule-7: Recommended first-version maximum request size is 1 GiB.
CommonRequestRules.rule-8: The existing upload endpoint preserves one uploaded file to one transcription job semantics; mixed-source batch transcription uses `/v1/transcription-jobs/batch`.

SharedEnums.enum: queued, running, cancel_requested, succeeded, failed, canceled
transcript_plain, transcript_segmented_markdown, transcript_docx, report_markdown, report_docx, deep_research_markdown, execution_log, agent_result_json, source_manifest_json, batch_diagnostics_json

Endpoint.purpose: Create one transcription job per uploaded file.

fields.field: files binary required one or more
display_name optional only when exactly one file is uploaded
delivery_webhook_url optional required when delivery_strategy equals webhook

Endpoint.response: 202 Accepted always returns jobs array, even for one file.

behavior.rule: Multiple uploaded files create one transcription job per file.
Uploaded files are persisted to MinIO before queueing work.
Reject empty file parts and ambiguous display_name usage for multi-file upload.

Endpoint.purpose: Create one batch transcription aggregate root from a mixed source basket.

fields.field: source_manifest JSON required with manifest_version batch-transcription.v1
files binary optional map of multipart parts referenced by uploaded-file sources
delivery_webhook_url optional required when delivery_strategy equals webhook

Endpoint.response: 202 Accepted returns the root aggregate job snapshot, not an array.

behavior.rule: API validates stable unique ordered_source_labels and source map keys.
API persists each uploaded-file source as its own source object and validates URL sources without requiring a source object.
API creates one root aggregate transcription job and one per-source transcription child per manifest source.
Per-source children are enqueued as `job:transcription.run`; the root is later enqueued as `job:transcription.aggregate` when the completion policy permits aggregation.
Root aggregate success requires transcript_plain, transcript_segmented_markdown, transcript_docx, source_manifest_json, and batch_diagnostics_json.
Batch flow does not change the semantics of POST /v1/transcription-jobs or the single URL flow.

Endpoint.purpose: Create an API-owned pre-submit batch draft for Telegram or another adapter.

fields.field: owner object required with owner_type and Telegram chat/user scope when owner_type is telegram

Endpoint.response: 201 Created returns draft snapshot with draft_id, version, owner, status, ordered items, and callback-safe state.

behavior.rule: New drafts start with status open and version 1.
Draft creation does not enqueue worker tasks and does not create jobs.

Endpoint.purpose: Read the authoritative draft snapshot for button rendering or stale-callback diagnosis.
Endpoint.rule: Caller must supply owner scope; owner mismatch returns a stable error and never leaks draft contents.

Endpoint.purpose: Add one uploaded-file, Telegram-upload, YouTube URL, or external URL item to an open draft.

request-fields.field: item required with source_kind, display_name optional, and API-owned uploaded_source_ref or URL data

behavior.rule: API rejects version mismatch, submitted draft, expired draft, canceled draft, and owner mismatch with explicit stale-button outcomes.

Successful upload add stores the file body through the API into the source object store at add-item time, strips transient body bytes from the returned draft item, appends a stable source_label, and increments version.
Successful URL add persists URL metadata only and increments version without creating worker tasks.

Endpoint.purpose: Remove one item from an open draft using optimistic version checks.

behavior.rule: Removal preserves remaining item order after compaction and increments version.
Stale, terminal, or wrong-owner callbacks return explicit errors instead of mutating a current chat/user basket.

Endpoint.purpose: Remove all items from an open draft while preserving the draft identity for already-rendered buttons.

behavior.rule: Clear increments version and returns the empty draft snapshot.
A stale clear callback must report version_conflict rather than clearing an unrelated current basket.

Endpoint.purpose: Atomically promote an open draft to the existing batch transcription root/children/aggregate graph.

request-fields.field: expected_version required from Telegram callback data

Endpoint.response: 202 Accepted returns submitted draft snapshot plus the root aggregate job snapshot or existing submitted root job.

behavior.rule: Submit validates owner, status open, version match, non-empty ordered items, and source availability inside one transaction.

Submit creates the same source_manifest shape as `/v1/transcription-jobs/batch`, persists the root source set, creates one aggregate root job plus per-source children, enqueues per-source transcription tasks, marks the draft submitted, stores submitted_root_job_id, and increments version atomically.
Repeated submit for an already submitted draft returns the same submitted_root_job_id or submitted draft response and never creates a second root job.
Submitted, expired, canceled, version-conflict, and owner-mismatch callbacks produce explicit responses suitable for Telegram stale-button copy.

Endpoint.purpose: Create transcription job from supported YouTube URL.

validation.rule: Allowed hosts are youtube.com, www.youtube.com, m.youtube.com, and youtu.be.

Endpoint.response: 202 Accepted with the same job envelope shape as upload flow.

Endpoint.purpose: Return authoritative job snapshot with artifacts, children, and lineage.
List jobs for local operational UI and adapters.
Create or reuse canonical report child job.
Endpoint.rule: Repeated create requests return existing canonical child unless explicit retry is used.

Endpoint.purpose: Create or reuse canonical deep-research child job.

behavior.rule: If job is queued and not claimed, API may finalize directly as canceled.
If job is running, API sets cancel_requested and worker must observe it cooperatively.
If job is terminal, API returns current job unchanged.

Endpoint.purpose: Create new retry row linked by retry_of_job_id.

validation.rule: Only terminal jobs may be retried.
Retry preserves root_job_id and parent_job_id when applicable and reuses source_set_id.

Endpoint.purpose: Resolve artifact metadata plus MinIO presigned download URL.
Endpoint.rule: Clients never construct MinIO URLs themselves.

Endpoint.purpose: Expose append-only event timeline for UI and MCP introspection.

delivery-rules.rule: Webhook is best-effort delivery, not the sole source of truth.
Failed deliveries are retried with bounded backoff and durable state.
Retry on network error, timeout, 408, 429, and 5xx.

SubscriptionModel.default: All connected Web UI clients receive the versioned event stream and filter client-side in local deployment.
SubscriptionModel.v1-rule: No subscribe message exists in v1.

WebSocketContract.MandatoryRule: Polling is the correctness path. WebSocket is a latency optimization. UI must re-fetch job snapshots through REST on version gaps or reconnect.

Lifecycle.step-2: Ask the API or internal service layer to claim the job.
Lifecycle.step-3: If claim fails because the job is already terminal or already owned, stop.
Lifecycle.step-4: Mark job running through the authoritative claim path.
Lifecycle.step-9: Finalize as succeeded, failed, or canceled.

endpoint.behavior: Atomically claims execution, performs queued to running, sets started_at, and returns execution_id, lineage, ordered inputs, params, and version.

endpoint.fields: execution_id and artifacts array with artifact_kind, format, filename, mime_type, object_key, and size_bytes
endpoint.behavior: Upserts artifact metadata by job_id and artifact_kind only after object upload succeeds.

endpoint.fields: execution_id, outcome, progress_stage, progress_message, error_code, error_message
endpoint.behavior: Validates required artifacts on success, writes terminal state, sets finished_at, and emits timeline, WebSocket, and webhook side effects.

ExecutionAuthority.rule-1: `execution_id` is authoritative control-plane state carried across claim, progress, artifact, finalize, and cancel-check calls.
ExecutionAuthority.rule-2: This state must be storage-backed; `jobs.started_at`, queue payloads, and worker memory are insufficient as the sole ownership signal.
ExecutionAuthority.rule-3: M-API-STORAGE owns the persistence boundary that makes this lifecycle reproducible for M-API-JOBS and M-API-HTTP.

TranscriptionWorker.reused-sources: current transcribers.py, transcript rendering in documents.py, source materialization logic from service.py
TranscriptionWorker.outputs: per-source jobs produce transcript_plain, transcript_segmented_markdown, transcript_docx; batch aggregate roots produce transcript_plain, transcript_segmented_markdown, transcript_docx, source_manifest_json, batch_diagnostics_json
TranscriptionWorker.progress-stages: queued, materializing_source, transcribing, rendering_artifacts, uploading_artifacts, aggregate_queued, aggregating_sources, completed, failed, canceled

AgentRunnerReportOperation.progress-stages: queued, loading_transcript, generating_report, rendering_artifacts, uploading_artifacts, completed, failed, canceled

AgentRunnerDeepResearchOperation.progress-stages: queued, loading_report, researching, writing_output, uploading_artifacts, completed, failed, canceled

ApiRules.rule-1: When cancel is requested, set cancel_requested_at.
ApiRules.rule-2: If running, set job status to cancel_requested and emit an event.
ApiRules.rule-3: If queued and still not claimed, API may finalize directly as canceled.
ApiRules.rule-4: If job is already terminal, return the current snapshot unchanged.

WorkerRules.rule-4: Check cancellation before artifact upload when skipping is still safe.
WorkerRules.rule-5: Check cancellation inside loops when processing chunked or long-running work.

SubprocessHandling.rule-1: Report-harness and deep-research subprocess handles must be tracked.
SubprocessHandling.rule-2: If cancel is observed, worker should terminate subprocess.
SubprocessHandling.rule-3: If subprocess termination succeeds, finalize as canceled.
SubprocessHandling.rule-4: If subprocess crashes during an authoritative cancel, still report canceled unless corruption requires failed.

RetryPolicy.rule-1: Retry creates a new job row and keeps historical rows immutable.
RetryPolicy.rule-2: New retry row references old row through retry_of_job_id.
RetryPolicy.rule-3: Retry preserves root_job_id and parent_job_id when applicable.
RetryPolicy.rule-5: Retry must not copy artifacts or job events from the source row.
RetryPolicy.rule-6: Create-child operations reuse existing canonical child rows instead of spawning siblings.
RetryPolicy.rule-7: If the caller wants a new report or deep-research run, retry the existing child job instead of calling create-child again.
RetryPolicy.rationale: Retry-as-new-job is cleaner for UI history, debugging, MCP introspection, and event auditability.

WebUi.item: subscribe to WebSocket and fall back to polling
surface delivery strategy choice between polling and webhook on create flows
trigger report and deep-research child jobs

TelegramBot.item: download attachments from Telegram when needed
create and mutate API-owned batch drafts for Telegram media groups and explicit baskets
submit API-owned batch drafts so the API promotes them through the batch transcription endpoint
register Telegram commands for start, help, batch toggle, basket view, and basket clear
expose a batch-mode inline toggle that switches between basket collection and immediate single-source processing
render inline callback data with draft_id and version for draft actions
create URL jobs through the API
poll for result readiness by default
send resulting artifacts back to Telegram users
present short readable `transcript_plain` artifacts inline as exact Telegram text when they fit the adapter limit
send an unavailable-artifact message with the result keyboard when `transcript_plain` resolves to a missing or non-file `text_path`
fall back to document delivery when an existing `transcript_plain` file is long, empty, or unreadable
expose buttons that trigger API actions

TelegramBotForbidden.item: treat chat/user keyed memory as the canonical basket state
process stale `basket:start` callbacks without draft_id and version

McpServer.item: expose create, read, list, retry, cancel, report, and deep-research operations
return artifact metadata and download handles

McpServerForbidden.item: talk directly to Redis, MinIO, or workers

WebUi.required-pages: Create Job, Jobs List, Job Details

required-capabilities.item: upload multiple files in one request
create one batch transcription root from multiple files or mixed file plus URL sources when explicitly selected

late-wave-admin-capabilities.item: re-resolve fresh download link for expired artifacts
manual rerun via explicit retry actions

WebUi.recommended-stack: React, TypeScript, Vite, TanStack Query, React Router

Mcp.recommended-stack: TypeScript, official MCP SDK, generated or thin typed API client from packages/sdk-ts

Telegram.recommended-stack: Python, aiogram, packages/sdk-py or thin internal API client

behavior-mapping.map: batch basket to /v1/batch-drafts create, add item, remove item, clear, get, and submit endpoints before the API promotes it to POST /v1/transcription-jobs/batch
batch toggle command or button to adapter-local presentation mode only; durable draft state remains API-owned
short readable `transcript_plain` artifact to exact inline Telegram text with the result keyboard
missing or non-file `transcript_plain` text path to an unavailable-artifact Telegram message with the result keyboard
existing long, empty, or unreadable `transcript_plain` file to document delivery with the result keyboard
segmented transcript button to resolve and send segmented artifact
report button to create report job and wait or poll
deep research button to create deep-research job and wait or poll

runtime-policy.rule: Telegram bot does not expose advanced per-job runtime knobs in v1.
Bot chooses its delivery strategy explicitly per request and defaults to polling.
Bot registers `/start`, `/help`, `/batch`, `/basket`, and `/clear` as the public Telegram command menu.
Batch mode defaults to enabled, can be toggled per chat/user, and when disabled the adapter processes a single source immediately or asks the user to select one source from a multi-source message.
Telegram media groups and user-curated baskets map to API-owned batch drafts first; sequential standalone media enter the batch flow only after an explicit user action such as Обработать вместе.

Every draft inline button carries draft_id and version; stale callbacks must display submitted, expired, canceled, version-conflict, or owner-mismatch copy rather than inspecting or processing any current adapter-local basket.

`TELEGRAM_INLINE_TRANSCRIPT_MAX_CHARS` is an adapter presentation guard, currently 3500 characters, chosen below Telegram message-size pressure so the bot can keep short transcripts readable in chat without changing artifact ownership.
Inline transcript delivery must use the exact gateway-materialized `transcript_plain` content; the adapter must not stitch batch pieces, re-render transcripts, or call a worker.

service.name: postgres
service.notes: local persistent volume and initialized database

service.name: redis
service.notes: no persistence requirement beyond local convenience

service.name: minio
service.notes: local persistent volume plus published S3 endpoint and published admin console on localhost

service.name: api
service.notes: depends on postgres, redis, minio; exposes HTTP and WebSocket; owns webhook delivery dispatcher

service.name: worker-transcription
service.notes: depends on api, redis, minio; includes ffmpeg and faster-whisper

service.name: worker-agent-runner
service.notes: depends on api, redis, minio; owns all report and deep-research AI operation execution

CpuNotes.note-1: Do not start multiple transcription workers by default if CPU is constrained.
CpuNotes.note-2: Keep report and deep-research AI concurrency isolated by `AGENT_RUNNER_HARNESS_CONCURRENCY`, not by separate worker services.
CpuNotes.note-3: Surface long-running progress in API and WebSocket so UI does not appear frozen.

EnvironmentContracts.Api: API_BIND_ADDR, API_BASE_URL, WS_BASE_URL, MAX_UPLOAD_SIZE_BYTES, WEBHOOK_DELIVERY_ENABLED, WEBHOOK_DELIVERY_MAX_ATTEMPTS, WEBHOOK_DELIVERY_BACKOFF_SECONDS
EnvironmentContracts.TranscriptionWorker: WHISPER_MODEL, WHISPER_DEVICE equals cpu, WHISPER_COMPUTE_TYPE, FFMPEG_BIN
EnvironmentContracts.AgentRunnerWorker: AGENT_RUNNER_HARNESS_CONCURRENCY, AGENT_RUNNER_CLAUDE_CODE_PROVIDER_API_KEY or AGENT_RUNNER_CLAUDE_CODE_PROVIDER_API_KEY_FILE, AGENT_RUNNER_CLAUDE_CODE_BASE_URL, AGENT_RUNNER_CLAUDE_CODE_MODEL, AGENT_RUNNER_CLAUDE_CODE_CONFIG_DIR, AGENT_RUNNER_CLAUDE_CODE_RUN_TIMEOUT_SECONDS
EnvironmentContracts.AgentRunnerForbidden: HOST_HARNESS_COMMAND, HOST_HARNESS_BRIDGE_URL, HOST_HARNESS_TOKEN, HOST_WORKSPACE_ROOT, CLAUDE_BIN, DEEP_RESEARCH_BIN, host.docker.internal worker execution, host-side broker execution as primary path, bind-mounted host executable, host path remapping

Map.from: retired workers/report and workers/deep-research dedicated runtimes

execution-specific code into Python workers; orchestration semantics into Go API; metadata persistence replaced by PostgreSQL, MinIO, and Redis integration
message parsing, Telegram downloads, client-side polling or wait flow, result delivery, and callback-to-API mapping only

MigrationMapping.MovedOutFromBot: direct execution, local job state persistence, direct report generation, deep research execution

contract.purpose: Provide the canonical API, WebSocket, webhook, enum, and validation contracts for the entire platform.
contract.scope: OpenAPI routes, shared schemas, public enums, webhook envelopes, WebSocket envelopes, and internal worker DTOs.

export-openapi.PURPOSE: Describe public and internal HTTP routes.

export-validationSchemas.PURPOSE: Describe request and response validation surfaces.

export-batchDraftSchemas.PURPOSE: Describe API-owned batch draft create, read, mutate, clear, submit, stale-callback, and submitted-root response shapes.

notes.note-1: This module must be complete before the first large implementation wave.

contract.purpose: Own PostgreSQL persistence, MinIO access, presigned URL resolution, and durable webhook-delivery records.
contract.scope: SQL persistence, authoritative execution-claim state, MinIO object IO, presigned URL generation, source and artifact lifecycle checks, and durable delivery bookkeeping.

export-persistJob.PURPOSE: Create and transition jobs with versioned state.

export-listJobs.PURPOSE: List authoritative job rows for polling, UI, and adapter reads.

export-getSourceSet.PURPOSE: Read the persisted source-set shell for job snapshot shaping.

export-listOrderedSources.PURPOSE: Resolve the stable ordered source inputs bound to a source set.

export-createBatchDraft.PURPOSE: Persist an API-owned open batch draft with owner scope and version 1.

export-updateBatchDraftItems.PURPOSE: Apply owner-checked optimistic-version add, remove, and clear mutations to ordered draft items.

export-submitBatchDraft.PURPOSE: Atomically mark a draft submitted and bind it to the promoted batch root job.

export-listArtifactsByJob.PURPOSE: Read canonical artifact metadata for one job.

export-listChildJobs.PURPOSE: Read canonical child jobs for lineage-aware snapshot shaping.

export-listJobEvents.PURPOSE: Read the append-only timeline rows for one job.

export-listDueWebhookDeliveries.PURPOSE: Read pending webhook delivery rows whose durable retry timestamp is due.

export-upsertArtifacts.PURPOSE: Register worker-produced artifact metadata after object persistence succeeds.

export-claimJobExecution.PURPOSE: Create the authoritative execution_id row and atomically claim queued work.

export-getActiveJobExecution.PURPOSE: Validate active execution ownership for progress, artifact, finalize, and cancel-check flows.

export-finishJobExecution.PURPOSE: Close authoritative execution ownership when a worker reaches a terminal outcome.

export-resolveArtifact.PURPOSE: Return artifact metadata plus a fresh presigned URL.

export-newMinioObjectStoreWithPresignCli.PURPOSE: Use the internal MinIO endpoint for object IO while signing browser-facing artifact URLs with the configured public endpoint.

notes.note-1: job_submissions and webhook_deliveries are mandatory early tables, not optional polish.
notes.note-2: The storage boundary also owns authoritative execution-claim state for `execution_id`; routes and workers must not infer that state from `started_at`, queue payloads, or process-local memory.
notes.note-3: Presigned artifact links must publish `MINIO_PUBLIC_ENDPOINT` when configured; do not rewrite Docker-internal MinIO URLs in adapters.

export-httpWebhookDispatcher.PURPOSE: POST webhook envelopes, persist 2xx/5xx/network attempt outcomes, and replay due deliveries through a bounded runtime loop.

notes.note-1: WebSocket is a low-latency UI channel; polling remains the correctness path.

notes.note-1: Workers must never bypass this module’s transition rules.
notes.note-2: Internal worker claim and finalize logic must consume storage-backed execution authority rather than reconstructing ownership from route-local state.
notes.note-3: Draft submit is orchestration inside the API control plane; it must not call a dedicated AI worker, bot-side batch business code, or adapter-local durable store.

notes.note-1: This module reuses current Python execution logic instead of rewriting ML behavior in Go.
notes.note-2: Worker extraction and compose launcher are implemented; remaining verification is real MinIO plus internal API claim/finalize integration.

contract.purpose: Provide the approved single AI execution worker behind the existing control plane without introducing provider-specific report, deep-research, Claude, or Codex workers.
contract.scope: Dispatch by `harness_name`, `agent_run` job-family execution inside the current control plane, report/deep-research operation envelopes, input artifact materialization through worker-only API artifact resolution, persistence through `jobs`, `job_executions`, `job_events`, and `job_artifacts`, normalized result summary plus redacted metadata in DB payloads, artifact-backed full logs or large outputs, explicit per-harness concurrency policy, and first-iteration container-local Claude Code execution through generated Anthropic-compatible settings JSON.

export-runAgentHarness.PURPOSE: Dispatch one `agent_run` job to the configured harness implementation selected by `harness_name`.

export-reserveHarnessLease.PURPOSE: Acquire a worker-local per-harness lease before dispatch.

export-agentClaudeCodeHarness.PURPOSE: Generate Claude Code settings JSON from explicit env values, resolve private request access, materialize input artifacts, and execute the container-local Claude Code CLI for `claude-code` harnesses.

export-persistAgentRunSummary.PURPOSE: Persist normalized result summary and redacted metadata to the existing control-plane job model while routing full logs and large outputs into artifacts.

export-persistOperationArtifacts.PURPOSE: Persist report_markdown, report_docx, and deep_research_markdown artifacts when requested by public operation envelopes.

target.tests: workers/agent-runner/tests and focused workers/common tests verified for the runtime and lease-boundary slices

notes.note-1: The approved shape is one generic worker keyed by `harness_name`, not separate workers per provider.
notes.note-2: The approved control-plane boundary is `agent_run` plus the existing jobs or executions or events or artifacts persistence model.
notes.note-3: For the first provider iteration, Claude Code uses container-local Anthropic-compatible settings generated from the `cglm`-style `AGENT_RUNNER_CLAUDE_CODE_PROVIDER_API_KEY`, `AGENT_RUNNER_CLAUDE_CODE_BASE_URL`, and `AGENT_RUNNER_CLAUDE_CODE_MODEL` env values; Codex and Anthropic Claude subscription login are out of scope.
notes.note-4: Per-harness concurrency must be configurable independently, for example claude-code=2.
notes.note-5: The generic runtime and worker-local lease-boundary slices are implemented with deterministic fixture dispatch, container-local `claude-code` dispatch, private request envelope resolution, generated provider settings, input artifact materialization, operation artifact persistence, and per-harness in-process lease limits. Compose service wiring is implemented through `worker-agent-runner`; live fixture-mode e2e, live external Claude Code provider smoke, and full report/deep-research agent_run orchestration tests are verified through the API, worker-control, Claude Code/GLM, and MinIO artifact flow.

notes.note-2: If `apps/web` does not exist yet, Phase-5 starts with a bounded app-shell unblock step before jobs or artifact features are implemented.
notes.note-3: The scaffold step must cover route and layout shell, env contract, and packet-local API client wiring so feature packets do not stall on missing frontend plumbing.

export-handleIncomingMedia.PURPOSE: Download Telegram media temporarily and submit it to the API.

export-handleCallbacks.PURPOSE: Map Telegram button callbacks to report, deep-research, retry, and segmented-artifact actions.

export-submitBatchBasket.PURPOSE: Submit Telegram media groups, buffered sequential media, and URL sources through API-owned batch drafts, then submit the draft for API promotion into one batch root.

export-renderDraftCallbacks.PURPOSE: Render Telegram inline callback data with draft_id and version for draft mutate and submit actions.

export-registerCommandMenu.PURPOSE: Register `/start`, `/help`, `/batch`, `/basket`, and `/clear` commands for Telegram clients.

export-toggleBatchMode.PURPOSE: Switch per-chat/user presentation mode between API-owned draft collection and immediate single-source processing.

export-deliverTranscriptResult.PURPOSE: Choose inline Telegram text for short readable `transcript_plain` artifacts, an unavailable-artifact message for missing or non-file text paths, and document fallback for existing long, empty, or unreadable artifact files without changing API or worker contracts.

notes.note-1: Default delivery strategy is polling for Telegram flows.
notes.note-2: If `apps/telegram-bot` is absent, materialize it inside the adapter packet while using the current `bot.py` and Telegram tests as the migration source; no separate shell packet is required.
notes.note-3: If `packages/sdk-py` is still absent during Phase-5, keep a thin packet-local API client under the Telegram adapter boundary and defer shared SDK extraction.
notes.note-4: `TELEGRAM_INLINE_TRANSCRIPT_MAX_CHARS=3500` is a Telegram UX limit only. It does not define transcript artifact size, API response shape, worker output policy, or batch aggregation semantics.
notes.note-5: Telegram must not treat in-memory BasketStore keyed by chat/user as canonical durable state; stale inline buttons are resolved by draft_id plus version through the API.

notes.note-2: If `apps/mcp-server` does not exist yet, Phase-5 starts with a bounded MCP app-shell unblock step before tool-mapping work begins.
notes.note-3: The scaffold step must cover server bootstrap, tool-registry shell, env contract, and packet-local API client wiring so the tool-mapping packet does not stall on missing adapter plumbing.

M-INFRA-COMPOSE.NAME: InfraCompose

contract.purpose: Own local Docker Compose topology, service dependencies, and environment contracts.
contract.scope: Compose topology, healthchecks, startup ordering, volumes, service env contracts, and smoke-run expectations.
contract.links: M-INFRA-COMPOSE, V-M-INFRA-COMPOSE, DF-001, DF-003, DF-006, DF-007

export-composeStack.PURPOSE: Start and verify the local platform stack.

M-INFRA-COMPOSE.depends: M-API-HTTP, M-WORKER-TRANSCRIPTION, M-WORKER-AGENT-RUNNER, M-WEB-UI, M-TELEGRAM-ADAPTER, M-MCP-ADAPTER

notes.note-1: Healthchecks are required before cutover because there is no long-lived legacy fallback track.
notes.note-2: After April 24 container-native verification, the obsolete `infra/host-tools/host-harness-*`, `infra/env/host-harness.env.example`, and `tests/test_host_harness_bridge.py` bridge residue was removed; the agent-runner path now remains container-local for the first Claude Code provider iteration.

DF-001.NAME: UploadedFileTranscription
DF-001.step-1: Client uploads one or more files to the API with delivery strategy defaulting to polling unless webhook is selected.
DF-001.step-2: API stores source files in MinIO, persists job_submissions, sources, and jobs, then enqueues one transcription task per file.
DF-001.step-3: Transcription worker claims the job, materializes source input, runs transcription, renders artifacts, uploads them, and finalizes the job.
DF-001.step-4: API emits versioned events, exposes polling reads, and schedules webhook deliveries only for jobs whose delivery strategy is webhook.

DF-007.NAME: BatchTranscriptionAggregate
DF-007.step-1: Client submits `source_manifest=batch-transcription.v1` plus referenced multipart file parts to `/v1/transcription-jobs/batch` with delivery strategy defaulting to polling unless webhook is selected.
DF-007.step-2: API stores uploaded-file sources in MinIO, validates URL sources, persists stable ordered source labels, creates a root aggregate transcription job, and creates one per-source transcription child per manifest source.
DF-007.step-3: Per-source transcription children run independently through `job:transcription.run` and persist their own transcript_plain, transcript_segmented_markdown, and transcript_docx artifacts.
DF-007.step-4: After completion policy permits aggregation, API schedules the root `job:transcription.aggregate` task; worker-transcription merges successful child artifacts in source-label order and writes root transcript_plain, transcript_segmented_markdown, transcript_docx, source_manifest_json, and batch_diagnostics_json.
DF-007.step-5: API emits versioned events, exposes one authoritative root polling snapshot with child lineage, and schedules webhook deliveries only when the batch root uses webhook delivery.

DF-009.NAME: ApiOwnedBatchDraftSubmit
DF-009.TRIGGER: Telegram draft callback with draft_id and version
DF-009.step-1: Telegram adapter creates or reads an API-owned batch draft, uploads draft item source material through API-owned references, and renders inline buttons whose callback data includes draft_id and version.
DF-009.step-2: Add item, remove item, and clear callbacks call the draft endpoints with owner scope and expected_version; API rejects owner mismatch, version mismatch, submitted, expired, or canceled drafts with explicit stale-button outcomes.
DF-009.step-3: Submit callback calls the draft submit endpoint with owner scope and expected_version; API validates that the draft is open and non-empty, then atomically builds the existing `batch-transcription.v1` source manifest and source-set rows from ordered draft items.
DF-009.step-4: Inside the same transactional boundary, API creates the existing batch aggregate root and per-source transcription children, enqueues per-source `job:transcription.run` tasks, marks the draft submitted, stores submitted_root_job_id, and increments version.
DF-009.step-5: Repeated submit for a submitted draft returns the same root job or submitted draft snapshot, while stale buttons for submitted, expired, canceled, version-conflict, or owner-mismatch states produce user-facing stale responses and never process a current unrelated adapter basket.
DF-009.evidence: V-M-CONTRACTS, V-M-API-STORAGE, V-M-API-JOBS, V-M-API-HTTP, V-M-TELEGRAM-ADAPTER, DF-007
DF-009.implementation-evidence: Implemented surfaces are `apps/api/internal/api/api.go`, `apps/api/internal/storage/batch_drafts.go`, `apps/api/internal/jobs/jobs.go`, `src/media_analysis_platform/bot.py`, and `apps/telegram-bot/src/telegram_adapter/gateway.py`. Storage persists uploaded draft bodies into the API-owned source bucket during add-item, jobs submit builds the existing `batch-transcription.v1` root/children/aggregate graph from ordered draft items, Telegram downloads files only to transient local paths before uploading them into API draft items, and old `basket:*` callbacks answer as stale without processing local/current basket state.

DF-008.NAME: TelegramInlineTranscriptPresentation
DF-008.TRIGGER: Telegram polling observes succeeded transcription job
DF-008.step-1: Telegram adapter waits for job readiness through the API-backed gateway and receives a processed job whose plain transcript path points to the materialized `transcript_plain` artifact.
DF-008.step-2: Gateway materializes artifact content through the internal artifact download-access contract; the adapter reads only the canonical `transcript_plain` artifact content already produced by the worker and exposed by the gateway; no bot-side stitching, transcript rendering, AI worker call, or MinIO URL rewriting is allowed.
DF-008.step-3: If the artifact text is readable, non-empty after trimming, and length is less than or equal to `TELEGRAM_INLINE_TRANSCRIPT_MAX_CHARS` currently 3500, the adapter sends that exact text as a Telegram message with the result keyboard.
DF-008.step-4: If the `transcript_plain` text path is missing or not a file, the adapter sends an unavailable-artifact Telegram message with the result keyboard.
DF-008.step-5: If an existing artifact file is longer than the limit, empty, or unreadable, the adapter sends the transcript document with the same result keyboard.
DF-008.step-6: For batch roots, the exact aggregate `transcript_plain` artifact is sent or attached, so source-label boundaries already encoded by worker-transcription remain preserved.

DF-010.NAME: AudienceScopedArtifactDownloadAccess
DF-010.TRIGGER: client or server-side runtime resolves an artifact
DF-010.step-1: Browser, host, Web UI, or MCP clients call `/v1/artifacts/{artifact_id}` and receive the canonical artifact-resolution envelope with a public presigned URL generated from `MINIO_PUBLIC_ENDPOINT`.
DF-010.step-2: Telegram adapter, worker-transcription aggregate logic, worker-common clients, and worker-agent-runner call `/internal/v1/artifacts/{artifact_id}/download-access` and receive the same envelope with an internal presigned URL generated from `MINIO_ENDPOINT`.
DF-010.step-3: Consumers download the returned URL without rewriting object-store hosts, bucket paths, or endpoint schemes locally.

DF-002.NAME: YouTubeUrlToTranscript
DF-002.step-1: Client submits a supported YouTube URL to the API.
DF-002.step-2: API validates the URL host, persists source metadata, creates a transcription job, and enqueues it.
DF-002.step-3: Worker resolves transcript or media through YouTube adapters, generates transcript artifacts, and finalizes the job.

DF-003.NAME: VersionedEventDelivery
DF-003.TRIGGER: job state transition or artifact creation
DF-003.step-1: API writes a new job_events row whenever job version advances or an artifact is created.
DF-003.step-2: Web UI receives low-latency updates through WebSocket and reconciles with REST on reconnect or version gaps.
DF-003.step-3: If the job uses webhook, API persists a webhook_deliveries row and retries bounded delivery until success or dead state.

DF-004.NAME: TranscriptToReport
DF-004.step-1: API validates that the parent transcription job succeeded and contains transcript_plain.
DF-004.step-2: API reuses an existing canonical report operation child or creates a new `agent_run` child with `operation=report`, `harness_name=claude-code`, transcript input artifact refs, and expected `report_markdown` plus `report_docx` outputs.
DF-004.step-3: Worker-agent-runner resolves private request access, materializes transcript input artifacts, runs the configured container-local Claude Code harness, uploads report artifacts, and finalizes the agent_run job.

DF-005.NAME: ReportToDeepResearch
DF-005.step-1: API validates that the parent report job succeeded and contains report_markdown.
DF-005.step-2: API reuses an existing canonical deep-research operation child or creates a new `agent_run` child with `operation=deep_research`, `harness_name=claude-code`, transcript and report input artifact refs, and expected `deep_research_markdown` output.
DF-005.step-3: Worker-agent-runner resolves private request access, materializes transcript and report artifacts, runs the configured container-local Claude Code harness, uploads deep-research markdown and execution logs, and finalizes the agent_run job.

DF-006.NAME: CancelOrRetry
DF-006.step-1: API validates current job state and either finalizes queued jobs as canceled or marks running jobs as cancel_requested.
DF-006.step-2: Workers check cancellation between expensive phases and finalize as canceled when authoritative cancellation is observed.
DF-006.step-3: Retry creates a new lineage-linked job row and never mutates historical execution records.

Wave-0.name: Baseline Freeze And Acceptance SSOT
Wave-0.goal: Freeze current behavior so the migration has a hard acceptance target; the historical baseline is now carried by README, compose cutover runbooks, and verification evidence rather than separate Wave-0 backfill artifacts.
Wave-0.Phase-0-1: Record current supported flows: YouTube, Telegram voice, Telegram audio or video or document, media groups, plain transcript, segmented transcript, report, deep research.
Wave-0.Phase-0-2: Record current artifacts transcript.txt, transcript.md, transcript.docx, report.md, report.docx, and deep research final report.
Wave-0.Phase-0-3: Freeze acceptance tests and identify which current tests must remain conceptually valid after migration.
Wave-0.Phase-0-4: Freeze newly accepted architectural decisions including asynq, polling plus webhook strategy model, MinIO presigned downloads, multi-file upload, no legacy compatibility track, and late-wave admin operations.

Wave-1.name: Monorepo Bootstrap And Contracts
Wave-1.goal: Create the monorepo structure and contract package before implementation spreads.
Wave-1.Phase-1-1: Create repository structure and placeholder READMEs.
Wave-1.Phase-1-2: Write OpenAPI draft for create transcription job, get job, list jobs, create report job, create deep-research job, cancel, retry, artifact resolve endpoint, idempotency, delivery strategy and webhook fields, and multi-file upload shapes.
Wave-1.Phase-1-2a: Historical separate combined endpoint freeze; current active contract is `/v1/transcription-jobs/batch` with root aggregate, per-source children, and stable source labels.
Wave-1.Phase-1-3: Write WebSocket schema draft with event envelope and event types.
Wave-1.Phase-1-3a: Write webhook schema draft with envelope, retry semantics, lifecycle events, and failure handling.
Wave-1.Phase-1-4: Define shared enums for job types, statuses, artifact kinds, and source kinds.

Wave-2.name: Infrastructure And Go API Skeleton
Wave-2.goal: Bring up local infra and a compilable API skeleton with DB and queue wiring.
Wave-2.Phase-2-1: Add PostgreSQL migrations for sources, source_sets, source_set_items, jobs, job_artifacts, job_events, job_submissions, and webhook_deliveries.
Wave-2.Phase-2-1a: Materialize authoritative execution-claim persistence backing `execution_id` across claim, progress, artifact, finalize, and cancel-check paths.
Wave-2.Phase-2-2: Add MinIO integration and bucket bootstrap assumptions.
Wave-2.Phase-2-3: Add Redis queue producer through asynq by job type.
Wave-2.Phase-2-4: Add REST endpoints as stubs first and then as persistence-backed handlers.
Wave-2.Phase-2-5: Add WebSocket server with connection, event broadcast, and reconnect-safe snapshot rule.
Wave-2.Phase-2-6: Add webhook delivery subsystem with stored webhook metadata, background delivery loop, bounded retry, and event emission rules per job type.

Wave-3.name: Transcription Worker Extraction
Wave-3.goal: Move transcription execution out of the monolithic bot into a standalone worker.
Wave-3.Phase-3-1: Extract reusable Python execution library by moving transcription and document rendering logic into worker-common.
Wave-3.Phase-3-2: Implement worker claim and run loop: receive job_id, fetch ordered input metadata, execute per-source transcription for source children, and execute batch aggregate root merging through `job:transcription.aggregate` without reintroducing concat-first root transcription.
Wave-3.Phase-3-3: Preserve current output formats: plain transcript, segmented markdown transcript, transcript docx.
Wave-3.Phase-3-4: Add cancellation integration by checking the cancel flag before and after long work.

Wave-4.name: Historical Report Worker Extraction
Wave-4.goal: Historical dedicated report-worker extraction; this topology is retired and current report execution is owned by worker-agent-runner through `agent_run` operation envelopes.
Wave-4.Phase-4-1: Extract report generation package into the report worker runtime and shared document-rendering layer.
Wave-4.Phase-4-2: Implement report job creation in API with validation that parent transcription artifacts exist.
Wave-4.Phase-4-3: Implement report worker lifecycle: claim report job, read transcript artifact, run the configured report harness, upload markdown and docx, finalize.

Wave-5.name: Historical Deep Research Worker Extraction
Wave-5.goal: Historical dedicated deep-research-worker extraction; this topology is retired and current deep-research execution is owned by worker-agent-runner through `agent_run` operation envelopes.
Wave-5.Phase-5-1: Extract deep research package with minimal rewrite.
Wave-5.Phase-5-2: Implement API deep-research child-job creation with report artifact validation.
Wave-5.Phase-5-3: Implement cooperative cancellation around each phase and subprocess execution.

Wave-6.name: Web UI Delivery
Wave-6.goal: Deliver a simple operational React UI over the live API.
Wave-6.Phase-6-1: Scaffold app shell with routes, layout, and API client wiring.
Wave-6.Phase-6-2: Create jobs list page displaying job type, status, created time, and progress.
Wave-6.Phase-6-3: Create job details page showing status, progress, event timeline, artifacts, and child jobs.
Wave-6.Phase-6-4: Add create job form supporting per-file upload, explicit batch transcription, and YouTube URL submission.
Wave-6.Phase-6-5: Add polling and WebSocket integration using polling as fallback and WebSocket for fast updates.
Wave-6.Phase-6-6: Add actions for report, deep research, cancel, and retry.
Wave-6.Phase-6-7: Add delivery strategy controls with default polling, required webhook URL validation, and explicit explanation that each job chooses exactly one strategy: polling or webhook.
Wave-6.Phase-6-8: Add late-wave admin operations on job details page: delete job, delete artifacts, refresh expired artifact download link, explicit manual retry actions.

Wave-7.name: Telegram Bot Migration
Wave-7.goal: Replace direct execution in Telegram bot with API calls.
Wave-7.Phase-7-1: Add typed Python API client supporting create upload job, create batch job, create URL job, get job, list jobs, create report job, create deep-research job, cancel, retry, and artifact resolution.
Wave-7.Phase-7-2: Adapt message flow so the bot becomes an input parser, Telegram downloader, HTTP client, and artifact sender. Use explicit delivery strategy per request and default to polling. Map Telegram media groups and user-curated baskets to `/v1/transcription-jobs/batch`.
Wave-7.Phase-7-3: Preserve current UX: plain transcript immediate delivery, segmented transcript button, report button, deep research button.
Wave-7.Phase-7-4: Remove execution coupling so the bot no longer imports execution core directly.

Wave-8.name: MCP Server Delivery
Wave-8.goal: Expose the platform to MCP as a clean adapter.
Wave-8.Phase-8-2: Add core tools for create transcription job, get job, list jobs, create report job, create deep-research job, cancel, and retry.
Wave-8.Phase-8-3: Add artifact visibility through artifact metadata and download endpoints.

Wave-9.name: Compose Hardening And Cutover
Wave-9.goal: Make the new platform the default local runtime.
Wave-9.Phase-9-1: Add persistent volumes and init scripts.
Wave-9.Phase-9-2: Add healthchecks and prove compose live-smoke convergence.
Wave-9.Phase-9-3: Add local runbook documenting startup, restart, logs, and common failures.
Wave-9.Phase-9-4: Cut over the old single-process entrypoint by replacing it with a compose-only fail-fast message after compose smoke verification and functional parity checks passed.

Wave-10.name: Historical Container Native Harness Cutover
Wave-10.goal: Historical bridge-removal packet for dedicated report/deep-research workers; the current topology deletes those workers and preserves container-native provider execution inside worker-agent-runner.
Wave-10.Phase-10-1: WorkerHarness contracts and dedicated worker consumption are retired from the active runtime.
Wave-10.Phase-10-2: Report and deep-research execution moved again from dedicated workers to worker-agent-runner operation envelopes.
Wave-10.Phase-10-3: Dedicated report/deep-research images and env files are deleted from the active compose topology.
Wave-10.Phase-10-4: Current acceptance lives in the worker-agent-runner e2e and compose topology gates.

Wave-11.name: Unified AI Agent Worker Architecture Decision Freeze
Wave-11.goal: Freeze the approved generic `agent-runner` architecture contract, control-plane persistence boundary, container-local Claude Code provider boundary, and per-harness concurrency policy without starting implementation.
Wave-11.Phase-11-1: Freeze the `harness_name` dispatch contract, `agent_run` job-family ownership, and the control-plane summary or redaction boundary for generic agent jobs.
Wave-11.Phase-11-2: Freeze first-iteration Claude Code integration as container-local CLI execution with env-generated Anthropic-compatible settings; Codex and Anthropic Claude subscription login are out of scope.
Wave-11.Phase-11-3: Freeze per-harness concurrency policy and worker admission control without introducing provider-specific worker processes.

Wave-12.name: Unified AI Agent Worker Implementation
Wave-12.goal: Implement the approved `agent-runner` contract, `agent_run` control-plane family, container-local Claude Code provider integration, and policy wiring after the decision-freeze packet is closed.
Wave-12.Phase-12-1: Add the `agent_run` submission and persistence wiring on top of the existing control-plane model; verified by `cd apps/api && go test ./...`, `uv run pytest --no-cov packages/contracts/tests/test_contract_surfaces.py`, and `git diff --check -- apps/api/internal/api apps/api/internal/jobs apps/api/internal/queue apps/api/internal/storage packages/contracts` on 2026-04-24.
Wave-12.Phase-12-2: Implement generic `agent-runner` dispatch by `harness_name` with artifact-backed logs and redacted summary persistence; verified by focused worker tests, contract tests, API tests, XML lint, GRACE lint, and reviewer approval on 2026-04-24.
Wave-12.Phase-12-3: Implement container-local Claude Code provider settings generation, private request envelope resolution, and per-harness concurrency enforcement; verified by focused `workers/agent-runner` and worker-common tests on 2026-04-26.

Wave-13.name: API-Owned Batch Draft Implementation Evidence
Wave-13.goal: Refresh the canonical GRACE evidence after API-owned batch drafts, stale Telegram callbacks, and draft submit promotion were implemented.
Wave-13.Phase-13-1: Verify API-owned batch draft contracts, DB/storage behavior, HTTP route behavior, job graph promotion, Telegram stale callback handling, no dedicated AI worker topology drift, and compose PostgreSQL E2E evidence. Commands and results are recorded under `VF-001C`, `V-M-CONTRACTS`, `V-M-API-STORAGE`, `V-M-API-JOBS`, `V-M-API-HTTP`, and `V-M-TELEGRAM-ADAPTER`.

Wave-14.name: Telegram Batch Owner Concurrency Hardening
Wave-14.goal: Fix forwarded voice or media bursts that arrive as concurrent single-source Telegram updates so one chat/user basket maps to one API draft and one debounced summary message.
Wave-14.Phase-14-1: Add per-owner Telegram basket serialization around API draft load/create/mutate, current draft pointer updates, and delayed summary scheduling; prove the prior race with a concurrent forwarded single-source regression test.

Phase-1.name: Grace Foundation And Contract Freeze
Phase-1.goal: Prepare the repository for GRACE-driven implementation and freeze contracts before any large code move.
Phase-1.step-1: Convert the migration plan into canonical OpenAPI, JSON schema, WebSocket, and webhook contracts.
Phase-1.step-2: Define required runtime topology, services, and health expectations in a GRACE-governed shape.

Phase-2.name: Control Plane Foundation
Phase-2.goal: Build the minimum Go control plane that can persist jobs, enqueue work, and expose contract-compliant HTTP routes.
Phase-2.step-1: Add PostgreSQL migrations, MinIO integration, and authoritative execution-claim persistence.
Phase-2.step-2: Add asynq task publishing and retry policy wiring.
Phase-2.step-3: Implement job creation, child reuse, cancel, retry, and lineage rules.
Phase-2.step-4: Implement job events, WebSocket fanout, and persisted webhook delivery scheduling.
Phase-2.step-5: Expose public and internal worker routes.

Phase-3.name: Transcription Worker Extraction
Phase-3.goal: Extract the current transcription path into a standalone worker without changing user-visible artifact semantics.
Phase-3.step-1: Create shared Python worker API and storage helpers.
Phase-3.step-2: Move current transcription execution and transcript rendering into worker form.

Phase-4.name: Historical Report And Deep Research Extraction
Phase-4.goal: Historical dedicated-worker extraction is superseded; stable report/deep-research public contracts now create `agent_run` operation jobs.
Phase-4.step-1: Dedicated report-worker execution was retired and replaced by `operation=report` agent_run orchestration.
Phase-4.step-2: Dedicated deep-research-worker execution was retired and replaced by `operation=deep_research` agent_run orchestration.

Phase-5.name: Adapter And UI Cutover
Phase-5.goal: Move all user-facing entrypoints to thin adapters over the API.
Phase-5.step-0: Create the minimal `apps/web` scaffold, env contract, route or layout shell, packet-local API client boundary, and app-local test harness required before Web UI feature work begins.
Phase-5.step-0b: Create the minimal `apps/mcp-server` scaffold, env contract, server or tool-registry shell, packet-local API client boundary, and app-local test harness required before MCP tool-mapping work begins.
Phase-5.step-1: Implement local job submission, monitoring, artifact download, and admin details pages.
Phase-5.step-2: Replace bot-side orchestration with API calls while preserving Telegram UX.
Phase-5.step-3: Expose the same core operations through MCP.

Phase-6.name: Compose Hardening And Final Cutover
Phase-6.goal: Validate the full local stack, enforce health gates, and remove the old single-process entrypoint.
Phase-6.step-1: Harden compose startup order, healthchecks, volumes, and smoke scripts.
Phase-6.step-2: Verify full-stack parity against the frozen acceptance matrix before deleting the old runtime path.

Phase-7.name: Historical Container Native Harness Cutover
Phase-7.goal: Historical host-harness removal is superseded by deleting the dedicated AI workers and running all AI operation execution in worker-agent-runner.
Phase-7.step-1: WorkerHarness was retired from active runtime; worker-agent-runner now owns container-local Claude Code dispatch.
Phase-7.step-2: Report generation now uses worker-agent-runner request-access, input artifact materialization, and operation artifact persistence.
Phase-7.step-3: Deep-research generation now uses worker-agent-runner request-access, input artifact materialization, and operation artifact persistence.
Phase-7.step-4: Compose now runs worker-agent-runner and worker-transcription only for worker execution; dedicated AI worker services and env files are removed.

Phase-8.name: Unified AI Agent Worker Architecture Decision Freeze
Phase-8.goal: Freeze the approved generic `agent-runner` worker contract, `agent_run` persistence boundary, container-local Claude Code provider boundary, and per-harness concurrency policy without implementation work.
Phase-8.step-1: Approve one generic worker keyed by `harness_name`, the `agent_run` control-plane family, redacted-summary persistence boundaries, container-local Claude Code settings-provider execution, and explicit per-harness concurrency policy.

Phase-9.name: Unified AI Agent Worker Implementation
Phase-9.goal: Implement the already approved `agent-runner` architecture in code and contracts without reopening the frozen decision surfaces.
Phase-9.step-1: Add `agent_run_create` contract/API/storage/jobs/queue control-plane wiring for root `agent_run` jobs keyed by `harness_name`, request-only `source_set` semantics, idempotency reuse or conflict behavior, and redacted request metadata persistence.
Phase-9.step-2: Implement the generic `workers/agent-runner` runtime that claims `agent_run` jobs, dispatches through `harness_name`, writes `agent_result_json` and `execution_log` artifacts, and finalizes through the existing worker-control API.
Phase-9.step-3: Implement container-local Claude Code settings-provider execution plus per-harness concurrency enforcement; compose service wiring, live fixture-mode e2e, and live external Claude Code provider smoke are complete.
Phase-9.step-4: Collapse public report and deep-research creation onto agent_run operation envelopes, materialize input artifacts inside worker-agent-runner, persist report/deep-research operation artifacts, and delete dedicated AI workers from compose.

risk.name: Rewriting too much execution logic
risk.mitigation: Keep the Python execution plane, move code instead of rewriting for style, and introduce wrappers rather than reinventions.

risk.name: Premature Go rewrite of ML or report logic
risk.mitigation: Go owns the control plane only; workers stay Python.

risk.name: WebSocket state drift
risk.mitigation: Polling remains authoritative fallback and jobs carry a monotonic version field.

risk.name: Cancellation is inconsistent across job types
risk.mitigation: Define cooperative cancellation checkpoints per worker and test subprocess termination explicitly.

risk.name: Compose stack becomes fragile
risk.mitigation: Add healthchecks, startup order, runbook documentation, and keep local deployment assumptions simple.

risk.name: Big-bang cutover increases integration risk
risk.mitigation: Freeze behavior baseline early, move UX only after API and workers are correct, and require compose-wide smoke verification before replacing the old entrypoint.

DefinitionOfDone.criterion: API server owns all business logic orchestration.
Transcription runs in worker-transcription; report and deep research run only as `agent_run` operations in worker-agent-runner.
Telegram bot is a thin API client.
MCP server is a thin API client.
Web UI can create, observe, and manage jobs.
PostgreSQL is authoritative job state storage.
MinIO stores source files and artifacts.
Redis-backed asynq is used for queueing, while event delivery is driven from persisted job events inside the API.
Polling and WebSocket are both implemented.
Webhook callback mode is implemented for all job types.
Cancellation works across all job types.
Artifact downloads resolve to valid presigned MinIO links.
Multi-file upload through the API works.
Admin operations on the web details page exist in the planned late-wave scope.
Docker Compose can bring up the full platform locally.

ImmediateNextTask.historical-guidance: Implementation should start with Wave 0 and Wave 1 only. Do not begin with UI or Telegram refactor first.

recommended-order.item: add DB schema and asynq producer
only then add UI and adapter clients

ExecutionRules.rule: Do not start from the UI.
Do not start from the MCP server.
Do not rewrite Python execution into Go.
Do not add auth or quota logic.
Do not build direct client-to-MinIO uploads in v1.
Do not use WebSocket as the only status mechanism.
Do not expose arbitrary runtime tuning knobs in Web UI or Telegram bot in v1.
Do not treat webhook delivery as stronger than polling.
Do not implement retry by mutating a finished job row.
ExecutionRules.separation: Go API equals control plane; Python workers equal execution plane; UI, Telegram bot, and MCP equal adapters.

ExecutionPolicy.worker-owns: Only module source files and module-local tests inside the approved write scope for the active module packet.
